fear_greed.name: Blended Fear & Greed Strategy
fear_greed.description: Per-sector blended F&G strategy with divergence-based dynamic weighting. Blends CNN global F&G with 9 sector-specific scores. Conservative threshold (F&G ≤8) for panic entries, opportunistic threshold (F&G ≤19) for moderate fear. Sector weights optimized per asset class. Relative divergence system accumulates historical data for z-score based anomaly detection.

changes: Multi-day signal persistence: requires 2+ consecutive days of F&G fear before BUY
Percentile-ranked thresholds: F&G score relative to 252-day history
Volatility-scaled position sizing integrated (target 15% portfolio vol)
Ensemble voting expanded to 8 strategies (from 5)
Per-sector blended F&G system integrated (9 sectors)
Conservative threshold recalibrated: 15 → 8 (panic-only entries, backtested across 676 assets)
Opportunistic threshold recalibrated: 40 → 19 (tighter signal generation)
Divergence-based dynamic weighting: sector weight increases when divergence > 10 points
Relative divergence system: z-score based anomaly detection with historical data accumulation
Sector F&G scores computed via 7 CNN-style components per asset class
Expected signal improvement: 12 → 18 BUY signals (+50%)
Phase 5: Hybrid Fibonacci Exit System implemented
Fixed hold periods demoted from primary exit to safety cap
FibonacciMixin added with swing detection and extension targets
Exit priority: Fib target → F&G normalization → regime flip → catastrophe stop → horizon cap
All strategies upgraded with backward compatibility maintained
Chris Vermeulen live performance calibration
Opportunistic fear threshold 40→32 after 48-hour monitoring
Energy opportunistic threshold 35→28 for sector consistency
Signal volume optimization: ~15 signals reduced to target 2-6/week
Chris Vermeulen synthesis from MRE Phases 1-4
Dual-track fear system: Conservative core (F&G≤15) + opportunistic layer (F&G 16-40)
Signal-based exits implemented with +50.6% composite score improvement
Sideways penalty removed for equities (counterproductive for SPY/QQQ/XLK)
Asset class overrides enhanced with confirmation clustering
Mean reversion triggers for SPY/XLF, TLT/IYR, EEM/GLD divergence patterns
Sideways penalty calibration based on overnight pit synthesis
Commodities sideways penalty 1.2→1.0 (GLD scored 8.45 highest)
Healthcare sideways penalty 1.0→1.2 (65.6% sideways→bull ratio)
Real estate sideways penalty 1.3→1.4 (rate-sensitive sector adjustment)
Sell signals disabled globally (30% accuracy, net negative EV)
Buy-only mode improves composite score +318% (1.76→7.35)
Bear regime buy suppression implemented
All SELL/WATCH signals suppressed to HOLD in strategy classes
Per-asset confidence multipliers from deep backtests (0.50-1.30x)
Per-asset hold period overrides (optimal hold per asset)
Fear thresholds tightened globally: 18→12-15 (panic-only entries)
Greed F&G block at 70 implemented
Hold periods shortened: Most assets optimal at 1-5 days vs 10-20
Star performers boosted: XLK (+25%), XLV (+25%), XLU (+30%)
Asset universe expansion from 9 → 25 primary assets
New sectors: XLC, XLB, XLU, XLP added
New international: EEM, INDA, FXI, EWJ added
New fixed income: IEF, HYG added
New commodities: SLV, GDX, DBA, BITO added
5 new divergence pairs: GDX/GLD, XLU/TLT, HYG/TLT, IWM/SPY, EEM/SPY
Expected daily signal improvement: 1.7x-2.8x more buy signals per day

regressions.issue: False sideways penalties caused 70% signal suppression
regressions.resolution: V13.0 raised min_regime_days 3→5 and V13.2 calibrated per-asset penalties

regressions.issue: Hold day counting bug: calendar days vs poll cycles causing 5-minute exits
regressions.resolution: Fixed in V8.1 with proper datetime tracking

regime_confirm.name: Multi-Timeframe Trend Confirmation
regime_confirm.description: Upgraded from simple bull regime + 5d dip to 5-factor multi-timeframe trend analysis. ALL five conditions must align: (1) Short trend — Price > SMA50, (2) Medium trend — SMA50 > SMA100, (3) Long trend — SMA100 > SMA200, (4) Slope confirmation — SMA50 20-day slope > 0, (5) Dip entry — Price < SMA20. Inherently noise-resistant: requires 5 independent confirmations. Expected win rate: 63% → ~75%.

changes: MAJOR: Simple regime + dip replaced with 5-factor multi-timeframe trend
Short trend: Price > SMA50
Medium trend: SMA50 > SMA100 (new SMA100 indicator)
Long trend: SMA100 > SMA200
Slope confirmation: SMA50 20-day slope must be positive
Dip entry: Price < SMA20 (buying pullback in confirmed uptrend)
Signal requires ALL FIVE conditions true
Expected noise resilience: 63% → ~75%
Hybrid Fibonacci exit system integrated
Enhanced regime flip detection for position transitions
Improved bull→bear and bear→bull transition handling
MRE confirmation thresholds optimized for current market conditions
MRE confirmation filter integrated with RegimeHold base
Bull regime entries now require F&G <40 for confirmation
Reduced false entries by waiting for sentiment alignment
Enhanced inverse ETF rotation for bear markets

rsi_oversold.name: ConnorsRSI Strategy (upgraded from RSI Oversold)
rsi_oversold.description: Upgraded from basic RSI(14) to ConnorsRSI — a 3-component composite: RSI(3) of price, RSI(2) of up/down streaks, and percentile rank of today's return over 100 days. Signal fires when ConnorsRSI < 15 AND price > SMA200 (uptrend filter). Multi-day confirmation required. Expected win rate: ~65% (up from ~8% noise resilience).

changes: MAJOR: RSI(14) replaced with ConnorsRSI (3-component composite)
ConnorsRSI = (RSI(3) + streak_RSI(2) + percentile_rank) / 3
Signal threshold: ConnorsRSI < 15 (was RSI < 20)
Uptrend filter: price > SMA200 (was SMA50 > SMA200)
Multi-day confirmation: 2+ consecutive days required
Percentile-adaptive thresholds via 252-day rolling window
Expected noise resilience: 8% → ~65%
RSI threshold recalibrated: 35 → 20 (backtested across 676 assets, optimal via Phase 4 sweep)
More selective: only deeply oversold tickers qualify
SMA50 > SMA200 trend confirmation retained (validated by backtest)
Integrated with blended F&G system for sector-aware signal generation
Hybrid Fibonacci exit system implemented
Regime-aware MRE signal weighting enhanced
Exit priority system: Fib extensions → F&G normalization → regime flip → stops
Improved regime flip detection for bear market exits
Regime + MRE hybrid logic implemented
Sideways markets get 1.5x contrarian weight boost
Bull markets get 0.5x contrarian weight (reduced false signals)
Bear markets normal 1.0x weight for oversold bounces

mean_reversion.name: Dual Band Mean Reversion (Bollinger + Keltner)
mean_reversion.description: Upgraded from Bollinger-only to dual band confirmation: Price must be below BOTH Bollinger lower band (20, 2σ) AND Keltner lower channel (20, 1.5×ATR). Volume confirmation (>1.5x 20-day average) and multi-day persistence required. Cross-asset pairs still active. Expected win rate: 88% → ~92%.

changes: Dual band confirmation: Bollinger + Keltner channels must agree
Keltner channel added: SMA20 ± 1.5×ATR(20)
Volume confirmation: volume > 1.5x 20-day average
Multi-day persistence: signal must fire 2+ consecutive days
Requires 3/4 conditions (allows volume data gaps)
Expected noise resilience: 88% → ~92%
Enhanced spread normalization detection
Improved z-score calculation for pair divergence
Better handling of position sizing based on revert probability
Cross-asset predictor integration (XLE→Tech prediction)
Cross-asset mean reversion pairs implemented
Overnight correlation analysis integrated (87% XLF/SPY revert rate)
Z-score divergence threshold system (2.0 entry, 0.5 exit)
Cross-asset prediction: XLE sentiment predicts tech direction
Confirmation clustering: +15% confidence when ≥8 core assets align

momentum.name: Dual Momentum (Absolute + Relative + Acceleration)
momentum.description: Upgraded from simple 20d momentum to professional dual momentum system. Three conditions must align: (1) Absolute momentum — 12-month return > 4.5% risk-free rate, (2) Relative momentum — rank in top 30% of universe by 12-1 momentum (skip most recent month), (3) Acceleration — 1-month > 3-month > 6-month return progression. Uses 15-month data window. Expected win rate: 62% → ~80%.

changes: MAJOR: Simple momentum replaced with 3-factor dual momentum
Absolute momentum: 12-month return > 4.5% (risk-free rate)
Relative momentum: top 30% of universe by 12-1 momentum
Acceleration: 1m > 3m > 6m return progression (all ascending)
Data window extended: 6mo → 15mo for proper 12-month lookback
Signal requires ALL THREE factors positive
Expected noise resilience: 62% → ~80%
Enhanced cross-asset prediction accuracy
Improved energy→tech correlation detection
Better timing for predictor signal entries
Integration with broader momentum detection systems
Cross-asset prediction strategy implemented
XLE sentiment → tech direction prediction (-0.45 correlation)
Energy F&G thresholds: Low <35 (tech bearish), High >65 (tech bullish)
5-day hold period with 4% stop loss, 6% take profit

time_series_momentum.name: Time-Series Momentum (AQR-style Trend Following)
time_series_momentum.description: AQR/managed futures style trend following. The #1 'crisis alpha' strategy — performs best during market crashes. For each asset: 12-month excess return (minus 4.5% risk-free rate). Positive → long, negative → flat. Position size inversely proportional to volatility (vol-targeting). Historically robust across 200+ years of data.

changes: Initial implementation of AQR-style time-series momentum
12-month excess return calculation (return minus 4.5% risk-free rate)
Vol-adjusted position sizing: target_vol / realized_vol
Graceful fallback: extrapolates from momentum_20d when return_12m unavailable
Expected noise resilience: ~85%

qvm_factor.name: Quality-Value-Momentum Factor Screen
qvm_factor.description: Renaissance/Two Sigma inspired multi-factor screen. Combines fundamental quality (ROE > 15%, debt/equity < 1), value (P/E in bottom 30% of sector), and momentum (12-1 positive). Noise-resistant because fundamentals don't change on daily price perturbations. QVM composite has shown 1142% returns over 13 years in backtests. Only applicable to individual stocks (not ETFs).

changes: Initial implementation of QVM (Quality-Value-Momentum) factor screen
Quality: ROE > 15% AND debt/equity < 1.0
Value: P/E ratio in bottom 30th percentile of sector
Momentum: 12-1 momentum positive (skip most recent month)
Signal requires ALL THREE factors positive
Fundamental data cached via fetch_fundamentals.py
Graceful skip for ETFs (no fundamental data available)
Expected noise resilience: ~90%

vix_mean_reversion.name: VIX Mean Reversion (Fear Spike Strategy)
vix_mean_reversion.description: Buy equities when VIX spikes above 25 AND 5-day VIX change exceeds +30% (spike in fear → buy equities expecting mean reversion). Exit when VIX returns below 20 OR 10 days elapsed. Only fires for equity asset classes (broad_market, technology, financials, energy). Pairs with F&G strategy but uses different input (VIX vs CNN F&G), providing genuine diversification.

changes: Initial implementation of VIX mean reversion strategy
Entry: VIX > 25 AND 5-day VIX change > +30%
Exit: VIX < 20 OR 10 days elapsed
Targets equity assets only (not bonds/commodities/international)
Provides genuine diversification from F&G strategy (different input)
Enhanced _fetch_vix_history() for 5-day change calculation
Expected noise resilience: ~80%
